title: PTD Today — Media
disclaimer: Informational only — AI-generated summaries; may contain errors. Verify with the original video.

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, dating app Hinge announced a $1 million fund to support social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to put on free or affordable events for young people. The move expands Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1 million fund for social groups.
Target cities: New York, Los Angeles and London.
Funds are for free or affordable social events for young people.
This is a global expansion of Hinge’s One More Hour initiative.
Framed as a response to increasing loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: Consider monitoring corporate-funded in-person event programs in major cities — they can create localized demand for connectivity and temporary power at venues.
Concentrated events in NY, LA and London may warrant coordination with local infrastructure providers for reliable on-site services.
Such social initiatives could signal opportunities for partnerships between tech platforms and venue/network operators supplying event-related IT and energy needs.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… This AWS Events session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an AI agent so the agent understands your business. The talk is an end-to-end demo emphasizing no graph expertise required.
bullets: Knowledge graphs can give AI agents understanding of company-specific information that generic models lack.
The session shows automatic construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Demo connects the generated graph to an AI agent so it can reason about your business.
No prior graph query or graph theory expertise is required.
Presented as an end-to-end build; links to a related episode on VectorDB vs GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
takeaways: If you plan to deploy enterprise AI agents, consider supporting pipelines that ingest documents for automated graph construction.
Automated graph-building can reduce reliance on specialized graph engineering skills when integrating knowledge into agents.
End-to-end demos suggest potential for faster proof-of-concept and deployment timelines for business-aware agents.
Ensure your infrastructure can connect stored/ingested document data to agent interfaces for practical integration.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US emphasizes that energy tax incentives are only one part of the sustainability tax picture and highlights less obvious credits such as state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation in packaging or processes. The core message is that viewing sustainability investments through a broader lens can unlock meaningful tax value for businesses.
bullets: Energy incentives are commonly considered but are not the whole story for sustainability tax value.
State and local abatements can provide additional tax support for sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may influence where sustainability investments are made.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation efforts, including reimagining packaging or processes.
Broadening the tax strategy around sustainability can reveal meaningful financial benefits.
takeaways: Assess state and local abatements as part of site-selection and project planning to capture additional tax value.
Include location-based incentives in financial models when evaluating new grid or data center sites.
Explore R&D tax credits for process or design innovations that improve operational sustainability.
Adopt a broader tax-incentive lens beyond energy credits to maximize the fiscal benefits of sustainability investments.

items.title: Copilot helps Christa find her inner star 🌟

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the video uses a sky-and-stars metaphor to show Copilot helping Christa "find the star within." It’s a short, uplifting promotional clip that invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Uses a sky-full-of-stars metaphor with a focus on personal discovery.
Positions Copilot as a helper in finding one’s "inner star."
Includes a clear call-to-action: download and try Copilot (link in bio).
Published by the Microsoft Copilot channel.
No transcript available; description is brief and promotional.
takeaways: Frames Copilot as a user-facing, empowerment-oriented tool—consider how AI assistants might improve staff engagement or user experience.
Emphasizes low-friction trials (download/try CTA)—evaluate pilot programs and simple onboarding for new tools.
Uses clear, human-centered messaging—use straightforward metaphors and calls-to-action when introducing technology to operational teams.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description, NVIDIA’s GeForce Garage video announces the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc Community Build Contest (u/JeffersonPutnam) and shows a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 system build. The description lists the full parts list link and key components including a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis, RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard, 32GB DDR5-6000 RAM, Corsair SF1000 power supply, and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam – SFF GeForce RTX 5090 build from the Summer of RTX community contest.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2 (small form factor).
Core components: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition GPU and Ryzen 7 9800X3D CPU on an MSI MPG B850I Edge motherboard.
Memory and power: 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000 and Corsair SF1000 power supply; cooling via Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
Full parts list available on PCPartPicker; host credited as Dwight (@_MightyPotato).
takeaways: The build uses a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition in a compact chassis—considerations around power delivery and cooling are relevant when integrating high-end consumer GPUs into constrained enclosures.
Use of a Corsair SF1000 SFX power supply and a 240mm AIO highlights the need to plan for adequate PSU capacity and liquid cooling options in tight builds.

Mini‑ITX platform (MSI MPG B850I Edge) paired with a high‑performance CPU (Ryzen 7 9800X3D) and DDR5-6000 memory shows component compatibility choices for high-density, high-performance workstation or bench setups.
The PCPartPicker parts list provides a direct reference for replication, validation, and compatibility checks for procurement or lab evaluation.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy AI solutions that unlocked entirely new revenue streams. They leveraged GenAI and Google Cloud's infrastructure to streamline operations, boost developer productivity, compress their digital transformation timeline, and expand what’s possible for the business.
bullets: TTEC and Google Cloud Consulting collaborated on rapid AI prototyping and deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to streamline operations.
Developer productivity was improved through the deployment.
The initiative unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Consulting support compressed the digital transformation timeline and expanded business ambitions.
takeaways: Cloud and GenAI capabilities can accelerate AI initiatives and create new revenue opportunities.
Engaging cloud consulting can shorten transformation timelines and speed time-to-value.
Improving developer productivity via cloud tooling reduces operational friction and delivery time.
Scalable, robust cloud infrastructure is important to support rapid prototyping and production deployment of AI solutions.
